document.source_title: WHO reports measurable health impact in 2025 amid transition to new strategy

claims.claim_text: WHO's impact was strongest in areas where its technical leadership and comparative advantage were fully leveraged.
claims.evidence_text: The report demonstrates that WHO’s impact was strongest in areas where its technical leadership and comparative advantage were fully leveraged.

claims.claim_text: An estimated 567 million additional people were covered by essential health services in 2025.
claims.evidence_text: an estimated 567 million additional people were covered by essential health services without experiencing catastrophic health spending in 2025.

claims.claim_text: An estimated 698 million additional people were better protected from health emergencies in 2025.
claims.evidence_text: an estimated 698 million additional people were better protected from health emergencies in 2025.

claims.claim_text: An estimated 1.75 billion additional people lived healthier lives in 2025.
claims.evidence_text: an estimated 1.75 billion additional people living healthier lives in 2025.

entities.name: World Health Organization
entities.description: The specialized agency of the United Nations responsible for international public health.
entities.evidence_text: The World Health Organization (WHO) today released its Results Report.

entities.name: Thirteenth General Programme of Work
entities.description: WHO's strategic framework for health improvements from 2019 to 2025.
entities.evidence_text: the report finds significant progress across all three 'Triple Billion' targets under WHO’s Thirteenth General Programme of Work (GPW13) for 2019–2025.

events.evidence_text: The World Health Organization (WHO) today released its Results Report.
The full report will be presented by the Director-General at the Seventy-ninth World Health Assembly (18–23 May 2026).

metrics.name: Additional people covered by essential health services
metrics.evidence_text: an estimated 567 million additional people were covered by essential health services without experiencing catastrophic health spending in 2025.

metrics.name: Additional people better protected from health emergencies
metrics.evidence_text: an estimated 698 million additional people were better protected from health emergencies in 2025.
